item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just want to know how the fuck they're running out of water

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always love a SCP with a well executed bomb drop at the end, and this was absolutely that. I very genuinely say, I had my finger on the -1 trigger because I was not at all interested in a story about a Dead Sea copy. You made those final few lines fill me with such a heavy sense of dread and interest that was completely redeeming. I believe there were some flaws in your earlier draft, but this injunction with the tale felt flawless to me and left me wanting more. Given, the tale was sort of a necessity to understand, but it was short and clear for the most part so they both got a happy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your feedback! I'll be sure to go over this and edit appropriately later today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The implications here are interesting (but seem to hinge on another tale that I've not read), but there are a number of minor issues that compound into me no-voting.

thoughts
– hide block

The lake is theorized to have formed due to excessive rainfall over the course of three months.

Why would they need to theorise? Palm harbour isn't exactly the middle of nowhere, surely the (pretty rapid) development of this new lake was catalogued and monitored.

The sequential listing of anomalous properties in this way feels very clunky to me, and the 2nd and 3rd anomalous properties are so minor by themselves and completely interlinked that I don't know why they aren't just part of the same thing.

cognitohazardous effect

This isn't a cognitohazard because it doesn't do anything to the observer (unless Im massively missing something here). It's just a visual anomaly or hallucination.

rainstorm lasting around ten minutes1 in duration which occurs bidaily over the lake and the surrounding half kilometer

This entire sentence feels very non-clinical. It's the worst offender I noticed but not the only place this is a problem. Also, bidaily is super ambiguous. Is it twice a day, or once every 2 days?

What about this town that's supposedly under there? Did there used to be a town in the place where this lake now is? Probably worth mentioning either way, especially since (after a very very brief skim) the tale seems to imply that there is a town there. And regardless, with the knowledge that dead bodies can penetrate this water barrier, why wouldn't they attempt some kind of exploration with a weighted dead body loaded with cameras or something?

As I said, all this points are relatively minor in the grand scheme, but they stack up. I imagery here is interesting enough (town trapped beneath a wall of water is a cool concept) that it will be a no-vote instead of a downvote, but I feel there are a lot of ways to improve this.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The rain didn't drown them. Rather, it drowned out their cries for help.

The second in an upcoming series.

I hope you enjoyed!

Image is registered under Creative Commons.

You can find more of my work here.
